extracted_items.content: Project name is 'taskflow-api' — a REST API backend for a task management app
Tech stack: FastAPI web framework, PostgreSQL database, SQLAlchemy ORM, Pydantic v2 for data validation, Alembic for migrations
User prefers uv for package management instead of pip or poetry due to speed
Project structure: app/ (main.py, models/, schemas/, routers/, services/, db.py), alembic/, tests/, pyproject.toml. Business logic should be in services, not route handlers
Testing requirement: minimum 80% test coverage mandatory for the project
Testing stack: pytest with pytest-asyncio for async tests and httpx for testing FastAPI endpoints
Deployment platform is Fly.io with Docker containerization using multi-stage Dockerfile
CI/CD: GitHub Actions pipeline that runs ruff linting, pytest with coverage check, and builds Docker image on main branch
Using ruff for code linting (replaces flake8, isort, and other linters)
User chose Pydantic v2 specifically for performance improvements, aware of breaking changes (@validator → @field_validator, @model_validator)

scenarios.title: Scenario 2: Heavy Dedup (Many Existing Memories Overlap)

extracted_items.content: Background extraction model is claude-haiku-4-5 (Haiku 4.5)

deduped_items.content: Background extraction model is claude-haiku-4-5 (Haiku 4.5)

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit project is deployed on Heroku
extracted_items.content: comfygit project is deployed on Fly.io (migrated from Heroku last week). Primary region: iad (US East). Containerized deployment with native Docker support.

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit API is currently on v2
extracted_items.content: comfygit API is on v3 (v2 fully deprecated last month). v2 endpoints return 410 Gone response. Migration guide: docs.comfygit.org/migration/v3
comfygit API v3 breaking changes: authentication moved to OAuth2 PKCE; workflow endpoint renamed from /workflows to /environments

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit uses Redis for job queue processing
extracted_items.content: comfygit uses PostgreSQL-backed job queuing with pgqueue Python library (switched from Redis). Processes ~500 jobs/day. Redis was deprecated as overkill for current scale.
comfygit database is Neon (serverless PostgreSQL), migrated from AWS RDS. More cost-effective for variable workload pattern.
comfygit workflow endpoint is /environments (renamed from /workflows in API v3)

extracted_items.content: Token storage must use expo-secure-store instead of AsyncStorage for encrypted token storage in React Native Expo app
Token refresh in React Native Expo app needs a promise-based mutex/lock to prevent race conditions when multiple requests fire simultaneously (especially on app resume from background)
Minimum supported iOS version is iOS 16
Minimum supported Android version is API 29 (Android 10)

Minimum supported iOS version is iOS 16
Minimum supported Android version is API 29 (Android 10)

extracted_items.content: JWT tokens for this project must use RS256 (asymmetric) signing, not HS256, for production security
Auth architecture uses stateless JWT authentication — tokens are verified by signature without server-side session storage
Existing auth config: AUTH_PROVIDER is 'internal', sessions last 24 hours, refresh tokens last 30 days, MFA is required, rate limiting is 60 requests/minute
JWT utilities are implemented in auth/jwt_utils.py with token creation, verification, and user ID extraction functions

extracted_items.content: Analytics service database technology: ClickHouse (columnar OLAP database). PostgreSQL was considered but rejected in favor of ClickHouse for its superior performance on complex aggregations and analytics workloads.
Analytics data pipeline uses Redpanda for event streaming (Kafka-compatible, chosen over Apache Kafka for operational simplicity). Redpanda feeds into ClickHouse via ClickHouse's Kafka table engine.
Analytics dashboard technology: Grafana. ClickHouse data source integrated via Grafana ClickHouse plugin. Grafana already running for infrastructure metrics.
Analytics service expected workload: a few thousand queries per day with complex aggregations.

extracted_items.content: CI/CD pipeline uses GitHub Actions with stages: lint → test → build docker image → push to GHCR → deploy to Fly.io. Deployment to Fly.io only runs on main branch.
Test matrix must validate Python 3.11 and 3.12 compatibility. Both versions are actively supported.
Frontend stack: Next.js 14 with App Router, TypeScript only (no plain JavaScript files allowed).
Frontend design system uses shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS. MUI and Chakra are explicitly rejected.
API rate limiting strategy: per-user tiered limits with 100 requests per minute for free tier and 1000 for paid tier.
Rate limiting implemented using Redis with sliding window algorithm. Redis instance is shared with job queue.
Feature flag naming convention: {team}_{feature_name}_{state} where state is 'enabled', 'disabled', or 'rollout'. Example: payments_new_checkout_enabled.
Feature flag management uses LaunchDarkly. The {team}_{feature_name}_{state} naming convention must be enforced within LaunchDarkly.

scenarios.title: Scenario 8: Very Short Window / Pure Greetings (Edge Case)

extracted_items.content: Architecture decision: modular monolith. Extract services only when there is a clear scaling need.
API technology: GraphQL with Apollo Server. Chosen to support multiple clients (web, mobile, third-party integrations).
Authentication: OAuth2 with PKCE for all clients. JWT tokens using RS256 algorithm. No API key authentication for first-party clients.
Search infrastructure: Elasticsearch. Chosen for complex full-text search requirements.
Caching: Redis for session cache and hot data. TTL configuration: 1 hour default for most keys, 24 hours for user profiles.
Background jobs: Celery with Redis broker. Periodic tasks via Celery Beat. No separate job service.
File storage: S3-compatible via Cloudflare R2. CDN via Cloudflare. Chosen to avoid AWS S3 egress costs.
Observability: structured JSON logs shipped to Grafana Loki. Traces via OpenTelemetry to Grafana Tempo. ELK stack rejected as too heavy.
Infrastructure as Code: Terraform for all cloud resources. Manual console changes are not allowed — all infrastructure must be in IaC.
Secrets management: HashiCorp Vault for all secrets. No secrets in environment variables directly. All secrets must be retrieved from Vault. This is non-negotiable.
Error tracking: Sentry integrated across all services.
Code review policy: PRs require 2 approvals. No self-merge on main branch. Main branch is protected. Feature flags required for all risky deploys.

extracted_items.content: Main GitHub organization for all work is github.com/acme-corp
Primary programming language across the organization is Python; TypeScript is used for frontend only
Staging environment at staging.acme-corp.com with separate database from production; production environment at acme-corp.com. Never cross-contaminate databases between environments
On-call rotation runs on a 2-week cycle; platform alerts are posted to #platform-alerts Slack channel
Always run tests after making code changes without being asked; do not skip tests
Commit messages must follow conventional commits format (type(scope): message) where type is one of: feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test. Always include scope in parentheses
Prefer short, direct responses. Use bullet points only when there are 3 or more items. Never include preamble
Code review priority order: security issues first, then correctness, then style
